_comment: Reference example for both the surface agent and validator agent. Every field is populated. This is issue #71774 — the ground truth POSITIVE case.
issue_title: matmul returns uninitialized memory for int64 tensors with inner dimension of zero
body_preview:
### 🐛 Describe the bug

This was found in opinfo tests. Observed in {windows CI test} and {internal @mode/opt}. Only seen on `dtype=torch.long`, not with ints.

Similar bugs are found in:
* `__rmatmul__`, int64
* ~`addmm_decomposed`, float16~ (turns out this is actually a jit-only issue)
* `linalg_multi_dot`, int64

repro scripts for other ops: https://gist.github.com/davidberard98/52fbd8c7...
surface_reasoning: Issue carries the 'module: correctness (silent)' label — PyTorch's explicit marker for bugs that return wrong answers without raising an error. The title names a concrete op (matmul), a specific dtype (int64), and an edge-case shape (zero inner dimension), all signals of an actionable, narrowly-scoped correctness bug.

linked_prs.title: Fix addmm_cpu for int64
linked_prs.abandoned_reason: PR was part of a ghstack stack. It was approved by COLLABORATOR ngimel and a pytorchbot merge was explicitly requested, but the GitHub API reports merged=false. Likely closed as a ghstack artifact when the stack was processed, with the fix either partially landing or being superseded. The issue remaining OPEN 4+ years later suggests the root cause was not fully resolved.
linked_prs.reviews_summary: COLLABORATOR ngimel submitted an APPROVED review on 2022-04-06 and then wrote '@pytorchbot merge this please' on 2022-04-07. COLLABORATOR lezcano confirmed the PR addressed the issue ('It is indeed!'). PR was closed 10 minutes after the merge request with merged=false — consistent with ghstack's branch-cleanup workflow rather than an explicit rejection.

blocking_signals: PR #75200 has merged=false despite approval and explicit pytorchbot merge request — whether the fix actually landed via ghstack stack merge is unconfirmed, and the issue has remained OPEN for 4+ years
confidence_justification: Step 1: Issue category — 'module: correctness (silent)' label marks this as a silent wrong-answer bug, the highest-priority bug class in PyTorch. (+0.20). Step 2: Reproducibility — exact repro script provided; two runs of identical matmul calls produce different garbage values proving uninitialized memory reads. (+0.25). Step 3: Triage quality — issue is labeled 'triaged', confirmed by two COLLABORATORs, and scoped to fbgemm backend. (+0.20). Step 4: Existing fix attempt — PR #75200 was written with 'Fixes #71774' in the body, approved by a COLLABORATOR, and a pytorchbot merge was explicitly requested. This is strong signal that a fix is feasible and welcome. (+0.20). Step 5: Fix scope — COLLABORATOR comment 'Happens with fbgemm only' narrows to a single backend; PR diff targets addmm_cpu, a single function. (+0.10). Step 6: Blocker — PR closed without confirmed merge, issue open 4+ years; some uncertainty about whether the fix was partial. (-0.08). Step 7: Age — issue is from 2022; code may have shifted but the fbgemm path for int64 zero-dim matmul is obscure enough to remain unfixed. (-0.05). Net confidence: 0.92.
ground_truth_delta: This IS the ground truth positive case, so there is no delta. For future findings classified against this reference: a finding resembling positive_71774 would differ by having a cleaner PR merge record (merged=true), a more recent creation date reducing staleness risk, or the fix explicitly confirmed on a specific hardware backend rather than 'fbgemm only'.
